title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
2-Step Security
2-step security is an extra layer of authentication added to an application. To log in to an application protected by 2-step security, a user must enter the one-time password from their registered 2-step security token.
2-Step Security Token
A 2-step security token is a device or application that generates a random one-time password that is recognized by applications protected by 2-step security.
AFT
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is a debit or credit transaction processed electronically through the CPAâs AFT system. A debit transaction is known as a Pre-Authorized Debit (PAD). A credit transaction is known as a direct deposit.
AML
Anti-Money Laundering.
Branch Number
A branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number, in conjunction with the 3-digit institution number, makes up the transit number.
Business Day
A business day is a weekday including Monday to Friday except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday.
Business PAD
A Business PAD is a pre-authorized debit drawn on the account of a payor for the payment of goods or services related to a business or commercial activity of the payor, including, but not limited to, payments between franchisees and franchisors, distributors and suppliers, and dealers and manufacturers.
Cash Management PAD
A Cash Management PAD is a pre-authorized debit drawn on the account of a payor for the purpose of transferring, consolidating, or repositioning funds between the payorâs account at one financial institution to their account at another financial institution, for the same business or closely affiliated businesses (e.g., transfers between a parent company and its subsidiary).
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. But
PaymentStream
AFT users can only be managed in User Management. CPS Admin Security Officers can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications (e.g., PaymentStream Direct).
Credit Transaction
A credit transaction is a credit payment item exchanged in an AFT File as defined in CPA Standard 005. Itâs also known as a direct deposit.
Data Centre
The data centre is a financial institution that processes AFT transactions for a group of financial institutions. Central 1 is the data centre for all credit unions in BC and Ontario and for some in the Atlantic provinces.
Debit Transaction
A debit transaction is a pre-authorized debit within the meaning of Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits, exchanged in an AFT file as defined in CPA Standard 005.
Direct Clearer
A direct clearer is a member of the CPA that meets the requirements of the Association and settles for clearing items through a settlement account at the Bank of Canada.
Direct Deposit
A direct deposit is an AFT credit transaction such as a payroll deposit.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Financial Institutions Branch Directory
The Financial Institutions Branch Directory identifies routing numbers and addresses for branches of all Canadian financial institutions. It is available free of charge from the Payments Canada website.
Financial Institutions File (FIF)
The Financial Institutions File (FIF) is an electronic directory of branches of deposit-taking institutions in Canada and is maintained by Payments Canada. It includes information to facilitate the processing and exchange of payment items between deposit-taking institutions in Canada.
FINTRAC
The Financial Transaction and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada.
Funds Transfer PAD
A Funds Transfer PAD is used when funds are drawn on the account of a payor at one financial institution and transferred to an account of the same individual at another financial institution (payor and payee are the same). A Funds Transfer PAD can include, but isnât limited to, registered savings plans, mutual funds, segregated funds, annuities, deposit accounts, cash accounts, and investment accounts.
Hard Token
A hard token is a physical 2-step security token.
Institution Number
The institution number is the 3-digit number assigned by Payments Canada that can be used on paper and electronic payment items to identify a financial institution.
Julian Date
The Julian date is the date per the Julian calendar. Dates are identified by a 6-digit number â0YYDDDâ (where YY equals the last two digits of the year and DDD equals the date as calculated from the beginning of the year, using consecutive numbers). For example, the Julian date for February 1, 2017, is 017032.
Lead Time
Lead time is a reference to the number of days before the due date that Central 1 must receive an AFT file. The lead time is generally two business days, but it may be shorter in some cases based on serviceability code â0â.
Notice of Change (NOC)
A Notice of Change (NOC) is a non-financial transaction created by the receiving financial institution. It informs the payment Originator of changes to the payment routing information.
Organization
Within
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT, the organization is the financial institution or business company to which AFT Originator IDs are associated or connected.
Originating Direct Clearer
The originating direct clearer is the direct clearer that delivers AFT files to another direct clearer.
Originator
An Originator is the financial institution or business member that creates an AFT file.
Originator ID
An Originator ID is the 10-digit number assigned by Central 1 that identifies the Originator of the AFT file.
Payee
The payee is the entity for which the credit transaction is intended.
PaymentStream
AFT
PaymentStream
AFT is a system that allows users to create AFT transactions and upload AFT files.
Payor
The payor is the entity for which the debit transaction is intended.
PCMLTFA
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
.
PCMLTF Regulations
The regulations passed under
PCMLTFA
, including:
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Suspicious Transaction Reporting Regulations
Cross-border Currency and Monetary Instruments Reporting Regulations
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Administrative Monetary Penalties Regulations
Personal PAD
A Personal PAD is a PAD drawn on the account of a payor for payments such as, but not limited to, charitable donations, non-member investment contributions, mortgage installments, utility bills, insurance premiums, membership fees, property taxes, credit card billings, lines of credit, loans, and payment for other consumer goods or services, but not a Business PAD, a Cash Management PAD, or a Funds Transfer PAD.
Pre-authorized Debit (PAD)
A Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) is an AFT transaction issued by a payee or member payee that is drawn on an account of a payor (e.g., mortgage payment, strata payment). See Debit Transaction.
Processing Direct Clearer
The processing direct clearer is the direct clearer that receives AFT transactions from an originating direct clearer.
RCMP
Royal Canadian Mounted Police.
Recall
A recall is a request to return an AFT transaction that hasnât been posted to the receiverâs account. A recall applies to individual credit transactions and to AFT files. Recalled AFT files can contain both credits and debits. Individual debits can only be recalled if Central 1 hasnât sent the transaction to the data centre of the receiving financial institution.
Receiving Financial Institution
The receiving financial institution is the financial institution that receives AFT transactions from another financial institution.
Recipient
The recipient is the individual who receives an AFT transaction.
Restricted Entity
A restricted entity is an entity involved in one or more of the following:
Money services businesses, including currency exchanges
Money transmitters and casas de cambios, including payment services providers
Casinos and gambling establishments
Internet gambling/gaming associated businesses and websites
Marijuana providers and/or distributors
Virtual currency exchangers and administrators
Serviceability Code
The serviceability code is a code on the Financial Institutions File (FIF) that represents the number of business days required to credit a payeeâs account with an AFT credit transaction on the date those funds are to be made available.
Soft Token
A soft token is a 2-step security token installed on the userâs mobile device as a mobile app.
Transit Number
The transit number consists of the branch number (five digits) plus the institution number (three digits). Each financial institution has a unique transit number (e.g., 80912010).
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
PaymentStream
AFT Application
Question 1
How do I update limits/contact information/email addresses for a
PaymentStream
AFT Originator?
Answer
Financial institution users with the âEdit Originator IDâ permission access
PaymentStream
AFT through
Client Centre>Applications>Production Applications>PaymentStream Direct>Perform Automated Funds Transfer
:
Select the Originator ID
Select the Action â Originator Information
Update the necessary limits/contact information/email addresses
Select Update
See the User Management Part for information on providing permissions.
Question 2
How are the monthly limits calculated?
Answer
Monthly limits are the total of all transactions settled in a calendar month and are reset on the first of every month.
Question 3
If a business only processes one file on the last day of every month, why would they exceed their monthly limit?
Answer
This can happen if the last day of a month falls on a non-business day and the last day of the next month falls on a business day. This means both files settle in the same month. Financial institutions need to consider this scenario for Originators with due dates falling on the last day of the month. Due dates on the first of the month arenât affected by this.
Question 4
How does dual authorization work in
PaymentStream
AFT?
Answer
Dual authorization in
PaymentStream
AFT is on a user, not the Originator. This means a user requires approvals, not the Originator.
Forced Approval
â If an organization with one or more Originators under it have an accumulative monthly limit of one million dollars or more, then all the users under that organization must be set to Yes under âRequires approval for their tasks/transactionsâ in User Management. There must also be users set up with the approval permission for the workflow to be correct.
Optional Approval
â If an organization wants to enforce approvals on individual users (but not all users under an Originator), then only those users who require approvals are set to Yes under âRequires approval for their tasks/transactionsâ in User Management. There must also be users set up with the approval permission for the workflow to be correct.
See the User Management Part for more information on providing permissions.
Question 5
Dual Authorization â Can I have it where it takes three users to release transactions?
Answer
The system is designed to only force two users to release transactions or upload files. There is no way to force the system to require three users.
However, for Data Entry, an organization can set up an internal process where one user creates the transactions, another user releases the transactions, and a third user approves the release. The approver will see/know who in their organization should have released the transactions and be able to effectively manage the workflow.
Question 6
Can
PaymentStream
AFT be accessed in any browser?
Answer
Yes.
PaymentStream
AFT can be used with all browsers including a MAC (Safari). This means users can also access
PaymentStream
AFT on a tablet or an iPad.
Question 7
Is there a timeout in
PaymentStream
AFT?
Answer
Yes. There is a 10-minute application timeout. This means a user needs to perform an action in the application to keep the session active. An action includes searching, adding, updating records, etc.
Question 8
How do I proceed if a user says they uploaded a file, but the file doesnât appear on the Activity Log?
Answer
If the Activity Log doesnât show the file validation, most likely the user didnât complete the process and they will need to upload the file again.
Question 9
How soon can a file be uploaded in
PaymentStream
AFT?
Answer
Files must be uploaded within seven calendar days of when they were created. Due dates of transactions in the file canât be more than 45 calendar days in advance.
Question 10
Can I update expired records in Mass and Multiple Changes?
Answer
No. Once a record is expired either from inputting an expiry date or using the One-Time (OT) frequency, it wonât be displayed in the Mass Changes or Multiple Changes. The purpose of an OT record is to take a one-time payment, which isnât the same as a recurring record.
Mass Changes and Multiple Changes are features for updating recurring records. Users will need to select a different frequency than OT if they require a record to be recurring.
Question 11
When does validated information appear in the File History reports?
Answer
Validated transactions are shown on the History reports after the transactions have been processed by
PaymentStream
AFT.
Question 12
If a business member gets an error message when attempting to access
PaymentStream
AFT when, previously, they were able to access the system, what could be the issue?
Answer
The unique AFT URL that business members are using to access
PaymentStream
AFT is a redirect. This creates an issue if a user sets up a bookmark incorrectly. A common error message from incorrect bookmarking indicates âNon-Authorized Userâ.
When bookmarking a redirect, business users must ensure theyâre bookmarking the exact URL provided by your financial institution and not adding to favorites throughout the access process. Setting up a bookmark correctly is done within the browser settings and is a different process for each browser.
If a business user gets an access error message when trying to access
PaymentStream
AFT using a bookmark, have them test access as follows:
Open a fresh browser session
Manually type the AFT URL into the address bar
Follow the access prompts
*Optional: If the business user is still experiencing an issue, have them try in a private/incognito browser.
Once a business user has been successfully accessing the site, it is recommended they add the AFT URL directly in their browser setting for future access.
PaymentStream
AFT Permissions in User Management (see the User Management Part for more information)
Question 13
Who can manage limits/contact information/email addresses for
PaymentStream
AFT Originators?
Answer
Financial institution users with the correct permissions to
PaymentStream
AFT Originators manage these features. Users require âRead Onlyâ and âEdit Originator IDâ permissions to the OID to manage these features in
PaymentStream
AFT. The âEdit Originator IDâ permission is only available to financial institution users, not to business member users.
For procedures on how to edit Originator ID information, see Question 1.
Question 14
Who gives permissions to financial institution staff to access/manage
PaymentStream
AFT Originators?
Answer
PaymentStream
AFT permissions are granted in User Management by UM Administrators at your financial institution. Once the permissions are granted, users can access
PaymentStream
AFT through
Client Centre>Applications>Production Applications>PaymentStream Direct>Perform Automated Funds Transfer
.
Question 15
Do financial institution staff automatically have permissions to all
PaymentStream
AFT Originators?
Answer
No. Each time an OID is added to
PaymentStream
AFT, a UM Administrator at your financial institution needs to provide staff with permissions to the OID in User Management.
Question 16
Why do we have to set up financial institution staff with permissions to
PaymentStream
AFT Originators (why isnât this automated)?
Answer
This needs to be managed internally by your UM Administrators, since the system has no way of knowing who at your financial institution will be managing, supporting, or accessing
PaymentStream
AFT OIDs. This is also for security reasons (mitigate risk) to ensure the appropriate assigned staff are accessing or managing the OIDs as intended.
Question 17
How do I see which users have access to a
PaymentStream
AFT Originator?
Answer
You will need to contact one of your UM Administrators as they manage access for all
PaymentStream
AFT users and permissions for your financial institution and its business members.
Question 18
Do financial institution staff require approvals to update the Originator Information for
PaymentStream
AFT OIDs?
Answer
No. Approval isnât required on the system when updating Originator Information (limits, emails, contact information). Your financial institution can set up an internal approval process if you want to have approvals on editing OID information. Alternatively, your financial institution users can audit changes made to Originator Information by creating an Originator Change report in
PaymentStream
AFT (Administration tab). This report shows all the changes made to the OID information screen.
Question 19
Do our business members have to use a token to access
PaymentStream
AFT?
Answer
Yes. All business users are required to have a token to access
PaymentStream
AFT.
Question 20
Can one organization use different token options for their users?
Answer
Yes. A token is assigned to each user, and users can choose whether they want a hard or a soft token.
Question 21
Can a business user have both a hard and a soft token assigned to them?
Answer
No. A user can only have one token, either a hard token or a soft token.
Question 22
Can we reassign a hard token to another user?
Answer
No. Once a token is assigned to a user it canât be reused for another user. If a user no longer needs the token, it must be disposed of, and an Administrator must remove access to the token from the userâs profile in User Management.
Question 23
When a business user has a new cell phone, what is the soft token process for the financial institution and the business user?
Answer
Your financial institution will need to deactivate the soft token in User Management and assign a new soft token to the business user. The business user will need to delete the old token from their authenticator app by highlighting the token and selecting delete. The next time the user attempts to access
PaymentStream
AFT, they will be prompted to register their new soft token. In the authenticator app, the user will choose to add another token and may need to select Scan a Barcode if not automatically prompted. As there is more than one authenticator app that can be used, the specific steps may differ slightly.
Question 24
How long does it take for systems to synchronize when changes are made to a userâs profile in User Management?
Answer
New Users
â Wait at least 30 minutes from the time the user is created in User Management to the time the user attempts to log in. It can take up to 30 minutes for the back-end systems to synchronize.
Providing access to or changing PS AFT permissions
â Same as above.
Passwords
â One to two minutes.
Tokens
â There is no sync delay.
END
